Petition For Reimbursement Of Former Admin. For Decedent'S Funeral Expenses Advanced For Estate And Petition To Compensate Former Admin. For Services Rendered To The Estate.
Off Calendar Oct-23-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Crisostomo Ibarra for Delia Ferrer (Petitioner). Dianna Davis for and with Carlos Ferrer (Respondent). Avid Alavi for Barbara DeVries.

Mr. Ibarra and Ms. Davis presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the issue of reimbursement of funeral expenses. Ms. Alavi stated that she leaves it up to the Court's discretion to decide what is appropriate. Ms. Alavi also stated that she sees a consent for fess and that they will do a petition for final distribution. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court granted the petition for reimbursement of funeral expenses in the amount of $8,094.00 (subject to receipt and review of a revised order). The issue of fees is to be addressed at a later date when the petition for final distribution is filed.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Motion For Determination Of Heirship Of The Estate Of Mary E. Ferrer Aka Mary Ernestine Ferrer
Off Calendar Sep-19-2013 Continued To Nov-20-13 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Heirship; Petition To Reimburse Former Administor For Decedent'S Funeral Expenses Advanced For The Estate And Etc.
Off Calendar Aug-07-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Barbara De Vries. Dianna Davis for and with Carlos Ferrer. Crisostomo Ibarra for Delia Ferrer.

Ms. Davis stated that she is in agreement with the petition to determine heirship based on the genealogy report and payment to Brandenburger & Davis, but not to reimbursement to Delia for advances of the funeral expenses. The Court informed Mr. Ibarra that objections cannot be filed in the form of a declaration or response, and advised Counsel that all issues related to reimbursement to Delia are to be addressed on October 21, 2013. Mr. Siracusa stated that there is nothing in Mr. Ibarra's declaration objecting to the petition to determine heirship, that it goes into issues regarding the creditor's claim and that it all refers to actions taken by Family members. The Court granted the Petition to Determine Heirship, etc. (subject to review of the order) except for reimbursement of funeral expenses which is to be addressed on October 21, 2013.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (126 18th Ave. S.F. Ca)
Off Calendar Aug-20-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Barbara De Vries.

The sale was confirmed at $1,355,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed July 17, 2012. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $1,090,000.00 is required.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy C. Garcia.

Petition For Revocation Of Letters Of Administration.
Off Calendar Mar-14-2012 Off Calendar

Petition For Revocation Of Letters Of Administration.
Off Calendar Feb-29-2012 Continued To Mar-14-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Revocation Of Letters Of Administration.
Off Calendar Jan-23-2012 Continued To Feb-29-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Barringer for and with Carlos Ferrer. Araceli Tovar appeared by telephone for Delia Ferrer.

The Parties agreed that it is in the best interest of the Estate to have a neutral private Fiduciary in place while they resolve the issue of distribution. The Petition for Revocation of Letters was continued to February 29, 2012 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the Parties to decide on a Fiduciary and for filing of an ex parte petition for appointment. Ms. Tovar will appear by telephone.

Judge Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.
